message:
fix(net,transport): QUIC RFC 9000 write-after-reset enforcement and transport fixes

quic_native/streams.rs: enforce RFC 9000 Section 3.1 — after a
RESET_STREAM has been issued, no further STREAM frames may be sent.
StreamTable::write_stream now checks send_reset before attempting to
consume send credit, returning SendStopped immediately. Previously,
write_stream only checked stop_sending_error_code, meaning a locally-
reset stream could still accept writes until the peer sent STOP_SENDING.
Update test to verify that reset_send alone (without on_stop_sending)
rejects writes at both the QuicStream and StreamTable levels.

quic/endpoint.rs: rustfmt PrivateKeyDer::try_from chain.

transport/mock.rs: fix Delay constructor to pass time_getter to
Sleep::with_time_getter instead of Sleep::new, ensuring mock delays
use the injected time source rather than the global clock. This fixes
non-deterministic behavior in tests using simulated time.

transport/stream.rs: remove trailing blank lines in test code, rustfmt
chained .await.unwrap() call.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
